perturbation.original_text: "Confidential Data" means (a) any Data disclosed by or on behalf of a party ("disclosing party") to the other party ("receiving party"), including, without limitation, (i) any materials, trade secrets, know-how, formulas, processes, algorithms, ideas, strategies, inventions, data, network configurations, system architecture, designs, flow charts, drawings, proprietary information, business and marketing plans, financial and operational information, and all other non-public information, material or data relating to the current and/or future business and operations of the disclosing party, and (ii) any information, material or data provided by third party vendors of the disclosing party; and (b) any analyses, compilations, studies, summaries, extracts or other documentation prepared by the receiving party based on the Data disclosed by the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: "Confidential Data" means (a) any Data disclosed by or on behalf of a party ("disclosing party") to the other party ("receiving party"), including, without limitation, (i) any materials, trade secrets, know-how, formulas, processes, algorithms, ideas, strategies, inventions, data, network configurations, system architecture, designs, flow charts, drawings, proprietary information, business and marketing plans, financial and operational information, and all other non-public information, material or data relating to the current and/or future business and operations of the disclosing party, and (ii) any information, material or documentation provided by third party vendors of the disclosing party; and (b) any analyses, compilations, studies, summaries, extracts or other documentation prepared by the receiving party based on the Data disclosed by the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'data' to 'documentation' in subpart (ii) of the definition of “Confidential Data” creates an in-text contradiction within the definition itself. This change implies that documentation from third party vendors is automatically Confidential Data, while other forms of data may not be, creating uncertainty as to what exactly is protected under this definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, Data shall not be, or shall cease to be, Confidential Data hereunder: (a) if such Data is known to the receiving party prior to disclosure thereof by the disclosing party; (b) after such Data is published or becomes available to others, without restriction and without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (c) after such Data becomes available to the receiving party from others having no obligation to hold such Data in confidence; or (d) if such Data is developed by the receiving party independently of any disclosure of such Data by the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text redefines 'Confidential Data' by changing 'data' to 'documentation' for third-party vendor information. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which specifies conditions under which 'Data' shall *not* be considered Confidential Data (e.g., if it's publicly available). The change in the 'Changed Text' makes third-party vendor documentation automatically confidential, regardless of whether it meets the conditions listed in the 'Contradicted Text.' This directly contradicts the conditions for exclusion of confidential status as stated in the 'Contradicted Text', specifically violating the condition (b) where the 'Data' is already in public domain.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees (a) not to disclose the Confidential Data; (b) use the same degree of care and diligence to protect such Confidential Data from disclosure to others as such party employs or should reasonably employ to so protect its own information of like importance (but in no event less than reasonable care); and (c) not to reproduce or copy the Confidential Data, in whole or in part, except as necessary for the evaluation or conduct of the Proposed Relationship.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise agreed to in writing by the disclosing party, the receiving party agrees (a) not to disclose the Confidential Data; (b) use the same degree of care and diligence to protect such Confidential Data from disclosure to others as such party employs or should reasonably employ to so protect its own Trade Secrets of like importance (but in no event less than reasonable care); and (c) not to reproduce or copy the Confidential Data, in whole or in part, except as necessary for the evaluation or conduct of the Proposed Relationship.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'information' with 'Trade Secrets' creates an internal contradiction. The initial phrase specifies 'Confidential Data', but the modified clause mandates the receiving party protect the disclosing party's 'Trade Secrets' with due diligence, without clarifying if Trade Secrets are equivalent to Confidential Data. If Trade Secrets aren't confidential data, then the clause becomes inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Confidential Data" means (a) any Data disclosed by or on behalf of a party ("disclosing party") to the other party ("receiving party"), including, without limitation, (i) any materials, trade secrets, know-how, formulas, processes, algorithms, ideas, strategies, inventions, data, network configurations, system architecture, designs, flow charts, drawings, proprietary information, business and marketing plans, financial and operational information, and all other non-public information, material or data relating to the current and/or future business and operations of the disclosing party, and (ii) any information, material or data provided by third party vendors of the disclosing party; and (b) any analyses, compilations, studies, summaries, extracts or other documentation prepared by the receiving party based on the Data disclosed by the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text referred to 'information of like importance,' which aligns with the broad definition of 'Confidential Data' in the contradicted text. The changed text narrows this to 'Trade Secrets,' which may or may not be equivalent to all 'Confidential Data' as defined. Because the contradicted text specifically defines 'Confidential Data' to include a wide range of information, and the modified text introduces 'Trade Secrets' without clarifying their relationship, this creates a potential contradiction. The receiving party's obligation now hinges on protecting specifically 'Trade Secrets' which might not encompass all forms of 'Confidential Data' as defined in the contradicted text, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The receiving party shall not have any rights or obligations respecting the Confidential Data other than those specifically set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party shall not have any rights or obligations respecting the Proprietary Information other than those specifically set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Confidential Data" with "Proprietary Information" without defining "Proprietary Information" introduces ambiguity, which is going to be contradicting the definition of "Confidential Data".
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Confidential Data" means (a) any Data disclosed by or on behalf of a party ("disclosing party") to the other party ("receiving party"), including, without limitation, (i) any materials, trade secrets, know-how, formulas, processes, algorithms, ideas, strategies, inventions, data, network configurations, system architecture, designs, flow charts, drawings, proprietary information, business and marketing plans, financial and operational information, and all other non-public information, material or data relating to the current and/or future business and operations of the disclosing party, and (ii) any information, material or data provided by third party vendors of the disclosing party; and (b) any analyses, compilations, studies, summaries, extracts or other documentation prepared by the receiving party based on the Data disclosed by the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Data,' which is a defined term. The changed text replaces this with 'Proprietary Information,' which is not defined. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Data'. The change causes a contradiction because the receiving party's obligations are now tied to an undefined term while the definition remains for the original defined term.

perturbation.original_text: As a result of the unique nature of the Confidential Data, in addition to all other remedies available, the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other extraordinary relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in order to enforce the receiving party's obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: As a result of the unique nature of the Protected Information, in addition to all other remedies available, the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other extraordinary relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in order to enforce the receiving party's obligations hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Confidential Data" to "Protected Information" creates uncertainty about the scope of protection. The agreement defines "Confidential Data," making it clear what information is subject to the non-disclosure obligations. Introducing "Protected Information" without a definition undermines the precision, and its replacement is unclear what the obligations it refers to, as the obligations referred to is about "Confidential Data"
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Confidential Data" means (a) any Data disclosed by or on behalf of a party ("disclosing party") to the other party ("receiving party"), including, without limitation, (i) any materials, trade secrets, know-how, formulas, processes, algorithms, ideas, strategies, inventions, data, network configurations, system architecture, designs, flow charts, drawings, proprietary information, business and marketing plans, financial and operational information, and all other non-public information, material or data relating to the current and/or future business and operations of the disclosing party, and (ii) any information, material or data provided by third party vendors of the disclosing party; and (b) any analyses, compilations, studies, summaries, extracts or other documentation prepared by the receiving party based on the Data disclosed by the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Data" to "Protected Information" introduces a term not defined in the contract, while "Confidential Data" is explicitly defined. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which defines "Confidential Data," because the changed text now refers to something undefined, creating ambiguity and undermining the original definition's purpose. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
